content:
Case 18:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 34
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: Follow-up for transfusion-dependent pancytopenia with persistent severe neutropenia and sequelae of disseminated fusariosis
- Vital signs: Temp 37.7 °C, BP 104/66 mmHg, HR 102/min (regular), RR 18/min, SpO₂ 94% on room air.
Setting: Outpatient hematology follow-up in a patient with longstanding PNH/aplastic marrow failure and recent disseminated fungal infection
Capabilities: You are seeing a profoundly cytopenic, heavily transfusion-dependent patient in the outpatient clinic. You can review symptoms, exam, current medications, cytopenia pattern, and recent cross-sectional imaging. You decide on urgency and scope of inpatient versus outpatient diagnostics and whether to proceed to repeat bone marrow evaluation and extended infectious work-up.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Complete blood count with differential"}}

Cbc Chemistry:
CBC: Hb 7.8 g/dL, WBC 0.9 × 10⁹/L, ANC 0.15 × 10⁹/L, Plt 18 × 10⁹/L; MCV 95 fL, MCH 31 pg.
Differential: neutrophils 11%, lymphocytes 64%, monocytes 19%, eosinophils 4%, basophils 2%.
Reticulocytes: 12 × 10⁹/L.
Coagulation: aPTT 35 sec, INR 1.3.
Chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 4.6 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, BUN 28 mg/dL, AST 41 U/L, ALT 44 U/L, bilirubin 1.6 mg/dL, LDH 328 U/L, uric acid 6.2 mg/dL, CRP 10.8 mg/dL, haptoglobin 21 mg/dL, ferritin 4950 ng/mL.
Immunology: CD4⁺ 380/µL, CD8⁺ 590/µL (CD4/CD8 ratio 0.64). Immunoglobulins: IgG 840 mg/dL (normal), IgA 68 mg/dL, IgM 42 mg/dL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Bone marrow aspirate and biopsy results (including cellularity, blast count, dysplasia, and clonal analysis)"}}

response.request: Bone marrow aspirate and biopsy results (including cellularity, blast count, dysplasia, and clonal analysis)

content:
'Bone marrow aspirate and biopsy results (including cellularity, blast count, dysplasia, and clonal analysis)' not available. Request a specific test or report.
Bone marrow aspirate/biopsy (cellularity, blasts, dysplasia, flow) is not available in this round. Consider requesting current labs (CBC/chemistry) or baseline imaging, which are available now.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Recent CT chest with contrast for evaluation of disseminated fusariosis and assessment of pulmonary infection extent"}}

response.request: Recent CT chest with contrast for evaluation of disseminated fusariosis and assessment of pulmonary infection extent

content:
Baseline Imaging:
2024-09-18 Abdominal ultrasound: Normal-sized liver (14.8 cm) with homogeneous echotexture, no focal lesions. Intra- and extrahepatic bile ducts not dilated (DHC 5 mm). Gallbladder poorly visualized (postprandial). Spleen 11.2 × 4.6 cm, regular margins, no focal lesions. Kidneys normal in size and morphology (left 10.8 cm, right 11.1 cm) with preserved corticomedullary differentiation. No hydronephrosis, no lymphadenopathy, no ascites.
2024-09-18 Echocardiogram: Sinus rhythm, preserved LV ejection fraction (55%), normal RV function, no relevant valvular disease or pericardial effusion.
2024-09-19 CT of the paranasal sinuses: Mucosal thickening in both maxillary sinuses (right > left) and ethmoidal cells, consistent with chronic fungal sinusitis. No fluid levels, bone erosion, or orbital involvement.
2024-09-20 CT thorax: Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules (1–1.5 cm), partly cavitary with halo sign, consistent with residual pulmonary fungal lesions. No pleural effusion or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Bone marrow aspirate and core biopsy with flow cytometry for cellularity, blast percentage, dysplasia assessment, and cytogenetics/cytogenetic clonal analysis."}}

response.request: Bone marrow aspirate and core biopsy with flow cytometry for cellularity, blast percentage, dysplasia assessment, and cytogenetics/cytogenetic clonal analysis.

content:
'Bone marrow aspirate and core biopsy with flow cytometry for cellularity, blast percentage, dysplasia assessment, and cytogenetics/cytogenetic clonal analysis.' not available. Request a specific test or report.
Bone marrow aspirate/core biopsy (including flow cytometry) and cytogenetic analysis are part of this case but are not available in Round 1. Consider requesting current labs (CBC/chemistry), history and physical, or baseline imaging which are available now.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Detailed physical examination findings focusing on mucosal surfaces, skin, and any signs of active infection or bleeding."}}

response.request: Detailed physical examination findings focusing on mucosal surfaces, skin, and any signs of active infection or bleeding.

content:
History Physical:
2024-09-15: The patient presented for follow-up with increasing transfusion requirements, requiring RBC transfusions weekly and platelet transfusions up to 3–4 times per week. ANC remained critically low at 0.1–0.2 × 10⁹/L. She reported skin lesions that showed gradual healing with postinflammatory hyperpigmentation and impaired vision with persistent blurriness and reduced acuity. She reported intermittent cough with occasional blood-streaked sputum and chronic nasal congestion.
Her general condition was poor with pronounced fatigue, weakness, and emotional distress. She appeared tearful and anxious during consultation, expressing significant fear of renewed long hospital stays, remembering the prolonged summer admission for disseminated fusariosis. ECOG performance status was 1.
Current medication: pegcetacoplan 1080 mg s.c. twice weekly, eltrombopag 150 mg 1-0-0, ciclosporin A 150 mg 1-0-1, voriconazole 200 mg 1-0-1, terbinafine 250 mg 1-0-0, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, cotrimoxazole 960 mg 1-0-0 (Mo/Wed/Fri), sitagliptin 100 mg 1-0-0, pregabalin 75 mg 1-0-1, sertraline 50 mg 1-0-0, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, filgrastim 30 Mio IU s.c. 2–3×/week as needed, epoetin alfa 40,000 IU s.c. once weekly, lorazepam 1 mg on demand for anxiety or insomnia.
Vital signs: temperature 37.7 °C, blood pressure 104/66 mmHg, heart rate 102/min (regular), respiratory rate 18/min, oxygen saturation 94% on room air.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Recurrent or progressive immune-mediated aplastic anemia (possible evolution toward myelodysplastic syndrome) with persistent neutropenia and sequelae of disseminated fusariosis in a patient with PNH, complicated by transfusion-related iron overload", "differentials": ["Transfusion-associated myelodysplastic syndrome", "Drug-induced (voriconazole, ciclosporin)-related neutropenia", "Active invasive fungal infection with marrow invasion", "PNH-related marrow failure"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Bone marrow aspirate and core biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics and clonal analysis", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Fungal antigen testing (galactomannan, \u03b2-D-glucan) and culture from sputum/blood", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consider tapering or discontinuing ciclosporin A and reassess marrow response", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Adjust antifungal therapy (increase voriconazole target trough or add liposomal amphotericin B)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Maintain/augment filgrastim dosing (weekly or more frequent as needed)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Initiate iron chelation therapy (e.g., deferasirox) to mitigate transfusion-related iron overload", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Consult for allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant evaluation if marrow failure persists despite immunosuppression adjustment", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Recurrent or progressive immune-mediated aplastic anemia (possible evolution toward myelodysplastic syndrome) with persistent neutropenia and sequelae of disseminated fusariosis in a patient with PNH, complicated by transfusion-related iron overload
differentials: Active invasive fungal infection with marrow invasion

treatment_plan.action: Bone marrow aspirate and core biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics and clonal analysis
Fungal antigen testing (galactomannan, β-D-glucan) and culture from sputum/blood
Consider tapering or discontinuing ciclosporin A and reassess marrow response
Adjust antifungal therapy (increase voriconazole target trough or add liposomal amphotericin B)
Maintain/augment filgrastim dosing (weekly or more frequent as needed)
Initiate iron chelation therapy (e.g., deferasirox) to mitigate transfusion-related iron overload
Consult for allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant evaluation if marrow failure persists despite immunosuppression adjustment

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Post–bone marrow work-up visit with new cytogenetic data
Capabilities: You now have bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, histology, and karyotype results. You can integrate hypocellular marrow findings, monosomy 7, and the known PNH clone, and reassess the diagnosis and risk profile. You decide whether standard immunosuppression remains appropriate or whether clonal evolution mandates a change in strategy and transplant planning.
Request additional information or solve.

content: {"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Refractory aplastic anemia with clonal evolution to low\u2011grade myelodysplastic syndrome (monosomy 7) in the context of a known paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria clone", "differentials": ["Drug\u2011induced marrow failure (e.g., voriconazole, ciclosporin)", "Acute myeloid leukemia arising from MDS", "Recurrent fungal marrow invasion"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Urgent HLA\u2011matched allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Discontinue ciclosporin A and stop current immunosuppression to reduce marrow toxicity until transplant", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Initiate iron chelation therapy with deferasirox to mitigate transfusion\u2011induced iron overload", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Escalate antifungal coverage to liposomal amphotericin B (or equivalent agent) to manage residual fusariosis", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Maintain filgrastim 30\u202fMIU subcutaneously weekly to support neutrophil recovery", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Obtain repeat CT of the chest to document resolution or persistence of pulmonary fungal lesions", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Refractory aplastic anemia with clonal evolution to low‑grade myelodysplastic syndrome (monosomy 7) in the context of a known paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria clone
differentials: Acute myeloid leukemia arising from MDS

treatment_plan.action: Urgent HLA‑matched allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation
Discontinue ciclosporin A and stop current immunosuppression to reduce marrow toxicity until transplant
Initiate iron chelation therapy with deferasirox to mitigate transfusion‑induced iron overload
Escalate antifungal coverage to liposomal amphotericin B (or equivalent agent) to manage residual fusariosis
Maintain filgrastim 30 MIU subcutaneously weekly to support neutrophil recovery
Obtain repeat CT of the chest to document resolution or persistence of pulmonary fungal lesions

Round 3 begins.
Setting: High-risk marrow failure and MDS transplant-planning discussion in the context of invasive fungal disease
Capabilities: You have full information including NGS (TET2, DNMT3A), detailed PNH clone quantification, prior IST response, and the course of disseminated fusariosis with ocular and pulmonary involvement. You must define the final diagnosis, antifungal strategy, and an allogeneic SCT plan, balancing curative intent against infectious risk and the patient's reluctance toward prolonged hospitalization.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content: {"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Aplastic anemia with clonal evolution to low\u2011grade myelodysplastic syndrome (monosomy\u202f7) in the setting of a persistent PNH clone and active disseminated fusariosis with pulmonary and ocular involvement.", "differentials": ["Acute myeloid leukemia arising from MDS", "Drug\u2011induced marrow failure (e.g., voriconazole or ciclosporin)", "Active fungal marrow infiltration"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Urgent allogeneic HSCT with reduced\u2011intensity conditioning (T\u2011cell depleted graft if available)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Discontinue ciclosporin and switch to high\u2011dose liposomal amphotericin\u00a0B (plus maintain voriconazole) to eradicate residual fusariosis", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Begin iron chelation therapy with deferasirox (oral) to mitigate transfusion\u2011related iron overload", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Maintain weekly filgrastim 30\u00a0M.I.U. subcutaneously to support neutrophil recovery", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Obtain a repeat CT chest and ocular imaging before transplant to document resolution of fungal lesions and assess risk", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Aplastic anemia with clonal evolution to low‑grade myelodysplastic syndrome (monosomy 7) in the setting of a persistent PNH clone and active disseminated fusariosis with pulmonary and ocular involvement.
differentials: Acute myeloid leukemia arising from MDS
Drug‑induced marrow failure (e.g., voriconazole or ciclosporin)

treatment_plan.action: Urgent allogeneic HSCT with reduced‑intensity conditioning (T‑cell depleted graft if available)
Discontinue ciclosporin and switch to high‑dose liposomal amphotericin B (plus maintain voriconazole) to eradicate residual fusariosis
Begin iron chelation therapy with deferasirox (oral) to mitigate transfusion‑related iron overload
Maintain weekly filgrastim 30 M.I.U. subcutaneously to support neutrophil recovery
Obtain a repeat CT chest and ocular imaging before transplant to document resolution of fungal lesions and assess risk
